title: CI: add API v2 tests
body:
API v2 has been quiet for a few days, and the test script is
actually passing. Let's take advantage of this opportunity
to get them running in CI.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body: so far changes LGTM are there any additional logs which would be helpful to collect for these tests (see `logcollector.sh`)?

Thanks for the reminder. There's one log file, full results of all HTTP requests, but I haven't yet had a need to look at it even in daily on-my-laptop testing. It's long, and I don't think it's worth preserving because the tests themselves provide a good dump on error.

@cevich the failing job is f30, and it's [failing](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/task/6687830147596288/logs/apiv2_test.log) because the system is cgroups v1, so my test expects to see `runc`, but it's seeing `crun` instead. Is this intentional, using `crun` on f30?
LGTM and seems to be passing everything but the problematic remote_client test
@TomSweeneyRedHat that's actually a weird and real failure; see my question to @cevich above (basically, f30 system using crun, so my test is confused, as am I)

> Is this intentional, using crun on f30?

Yes this was intentional, unless that's bad, then...errr...it was a mistake :grin:

(Meaning: I'm open to hearing arguments to change it)
Okay, decision is: F30 should be runc only.  Working on a PR for that (IIUC, should be small/simple change).
Yay - with Chris's #5342, this is now passing.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
